product_name: Dear Zoo Lift the Flap 40th Anniversary Edition
rank_rationale: #1: clearest UK flap classic for early naming and turn-taking at 4-6m.
include_rationale: Talk stretches past coos now, and animal flaps give a clear pause plus a reveal to name together. Keep turns short on the sofa so the book stays a chat, not a chew marathon. Stop while the look still feels friendly and close the covers.

rank_rationale: #2: shorter flap game than Dear Zoo; strong UK familiarity.
include_rationale: Waiting for Spot under a flap matches the new patience for a short reveal. One or two flaps per sitting is enough; celebrate the look, then close the book while it still feels friendly. Stay close on the sofa and keep the hunt short.

product_name: Brown Bear, Brown Bear, What Do You See?
rank_rationale: #3: rhythm and colour chorus for shared looking.
include_rationale: A repeating question gives tired parents a ready refrain when freestyle chat runs dry. Bright animals hold a longer look than newborn high contrast alone. Keep one animal per turn, then close the book while the look still feels friendly together.

rank_rationale: #4: interactive peekaboo without tiny parts.
include_rationale: Peekaboo is the game of the season, and sliders make the hide and find physical without needing tiny finger control yet. Stop after a few slides before frustration. Stay close on the lap and close the book while smiles are still easy.

rank_rationale: #5: classic UK touchy-feely entry; Usborne capped later.
include_rationale: Hands explore while ears hear the same refrain. Soft textures give a second job besides looking, useful when mouthing is the default. One patch per sitting is enough; close the book while the look still feels friendly on the sofa.

rank_rationale: #6: second Usborne touchy-feely; brand capped at two.
include_rationale: If Puppy already lives in the basket, Cat still works as a second texture set without a new story pattern to learn. Rotate when one is sticky with milk. Keep turns short and stop while the shared look still feels friendly.

rank_rationale: #7: publisher-primary faces book; Ladybird capped later.
include_rationale: Faces stay the favourite subject even as colour arrives. Hold it close during a feed break so a chat can ride on a familiar look. One or two pages is enough; stop while the shared look still feels friendly and put the book away.

rank_rationale: #8: second Ladybird Baby Touch; brand capped at two.
include_rationale: Animals give a second bold lane when faces feel done for the day. Keep pages slow: one animal, one sound, then pause. Close the book while the look still feels friendly rather than racing through every spread in one sitting.

rank_rationale: #9: rhyme energy; Puffin already used once for Brown Bear - brand is Puffin twice max.
include_rationale: Rhythm carries a longer listen than a single picture page. Use it when they are settled on your chest and ready for a short story pulse. Keep one verse, then stop while the listen still feels friendly rather than pushing a full chapter.

rank_rationale: #10: bridges talk and body naming; stronger fit also on parts-of-me card.
include_rationale: Song plus page makes body words stick without a talking to. Touch their knees while you sing; the book is the prompt, not homework. Keep one verse, then stop while smiles are still easy and put the book back on the shelf.
